title: Creating a copy of Main tasks only to auditing board
excerpt: I recently used an automation to create a copy of a task and move it to an auditing board for verification purposes.  However, the struggle I am running into is that when I try to apply it to other board with subitems, i…

posts.author: DaniD-AFS (Danielle Dearth)
posts.cookedHtml: I recently used an automation to create a copy of a task and move it to an auditing board for verification purposes. However, the struggle I am running into is that when I try to apply it to other board with subitems, it wants to change the automation to only allow me to select subitems. I don’t want to be spammed with completion of subitems when I just want to verify a project is completed and doesn’t require additional changes to automations, work instructions etc.

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @DaniD-AFS - would love to help but I’m afraid I didn’t really understand the issue you’re describing.

Can you explain how you would like the flow to work and what’s currently not possible?

posts.author: DaniD-AFS (Danielle Dearth)
posts.cookedHtml: The board template that our staff all uses has a mandatory sub-item requirement on it. When I try to apply the automation to any board with this template it will not allow me to select just to receive a notification when the main task is completed, rather only allows me to select sub-items. The purpose is to audit any closed items to make sure that documentation, system automations etc. are all updated if necessary. I wouldn’t want to be notified for the subtasks just the main but I’m struggling with how to work around that.

posts.author: garfield-labs (Snir Alon)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey Danielle, you can use Same Item Multiple Boards and share the items between the “High level board” and the project boards without their subitems. So only when a parent item will change in your main board, you’ll get a notification
